senses.definition: the compassion and sympathy that is shown to others

examples.x: Her endless acts of charity and compassion truly exemplify the milk of human kindness.
In times of crisis, his remarkable ability to show the milk of human kindness shines through.
The teacher's patience and understanding toward her students reveal the depths of her milk of human kindness.
Even in the face of adversity, she always manages to pour out the milk of human kindness to those in need.
His commitment to helping others is a testament to the endless reservoir of the milk of human kindness within him.

senses.definition: used to emphasize that a person is just a normal human being with similar feelings or weaknesses as others

examples.x: In times of crisis, the community came together, showing that they were not just neighbors but also flesh and blood, ready to support each other.
He may be a famous actor, but he's still just flesh and blood like the rest of us.
The superhero had extraordinary powers, but deep down, he was still flesh and blood with his own fears and insecurities.
The CEO's decisions impacted the lives of many employees who were flesh and blood, not just numbers on a spreadsheet.

word_list.pronunciation: tˈʌɡ ɔːɹ pˈʊl æt wˈʌnz hˈɑːɹtstɹɪŋz

senses.definition: to make a strong impression on someone, in a way that they feel a strong sense of love or sympathy for a person

examples.x: The heartwarming story of a rescue dog finding a forever home never fails to tug at my heartstrings.
The old love letters, rediscovered in the attic, brought tears to her eyes as they tugged at her heartstrings with memories of a bygone era.

senses.definition: to no longer have kind feelings for a person

examples.x: After being betrayed by her closest friend, she decided to harden her heart to protect herself from further emotional pain.
In his line of work, he had to harden his heart to the suffering he witnessed daily in order to maintain his professionalism.
Despite the heartbreaking stories he encountered as a counselor, he never hardened his heart and continued to offer compassion and support to his clients.
The film's protagonist initially tried to harden his heart against love but eventually succumbed to the power of affection.

senses.definition: sympathy, help, or encouragement that is barely enough to improve a person's situation or make them feel any better

examples.x: After failing the exam, the teacher's reassurance that "at least you tried your best" felt like cold comfort to the disappointed student.
The apology from the airline for the flight delay was cold comfort to the weary passengers who had missed their connections.
Hearing that the stolen items had been recovered by the police was cold comfort to the burglary victims, who had already lost their sense of security.
The doctor's words of "there's nothing seriously wrong" were cold comfort to the patient, who still suffered from unexplained symptoms.

senses.definition: in a way that is absolutely cruel and shows no sympathy or mercy of any kind

examples.x: The murderer committed the crime in cold blood, showing no remorse for his actions.
The spy executed the mission in cold blood, carrying out the orders without hesitation.
She broke up with him in cold blood, leaving him stunned and heartbroken.
The robber stole the cash from the register in cold blood, leaving the store owner terrified.
The decision to lay off employees was made in cold blood, causing anger and frustration among the staff.

senses.definition: to soothe someone who is injured, usually a child, by kissing the injured spot

examples.x: After she fell and scraped her knee, her mother gently kissed it better to soothe her tears.
When the child bumped his head, his grandmother would always kiss it better and tell him everything would be okay.
After her little brother stubbed his toe, the older sister offered to kiss it better to make him smile.
The loving gesture of kissing the puppy's paw made the young girl feel like she was kissing it better after a minor mishap.

senses.definition: used to convey intense sadness or empathy one feels for someone or something in a tragic or distressing situation

examples.x: When I heard about the devastating earthquake, my heart bled for the people affected by the disaster.
Her heart bled for the homeless man shivering in the cold, and she immediately offered him a warm blanket.
As she read the heartbreaking news about the refugee crisis, her heart bled for the families fleeing their war-torn homeland.
Seeing the injured animals in the shelter, his heart bled, and he decided to volunteer to help them find loving homes.

senses.definition: to finally talk about what has long been bothering or concerning one

examples.x: He couldn't bear the guilt any longer, so he confessed and got the truth off his chest during the therapy session.
John had been holding onto a secret for years, and he knew it was time to get it off his chest and tell his family the truth.
Sometimes, sharing your worries and fears with a trusted friend can be a great way to get things off your chest and find emotional relief.

senses.definition: used to ask a person to stop their unkind or indifferent behavior and offer some help or show some sympathy instead

examples.x: She pleaded with her landlord to have a heart and postpone the rent increase due to her financial difficulties.
The employee asked his supervisor to have a heart and grant him a few days off to attend his sister's wedding.
Seeing the homeless man shivering in the cold, she decided to have a heart and buy him a warm meal.
Have some heart Lucas!

senses.definition: in a similar or identical situation to someone else, particularly a difficult or unpleasant one

examples.x: Before criticizing her decisions, try to put yourself in her shoes and consider the challenges she faces.
I can't truly comprehend the stress of his job until I've been in his shoes and experienced the daily demands firsthand.
Having been in his shoes during a similar crisis, she offered valuable advice on how to cope with the situation.

word_list.pronunciation: wˈʌnz hˈɑːɹt ɡˌoʊ ˈaʊt tʊ ˌɛsbˈiː

senses.definition: used to show one sympathizes with and feels sorry for someone else's suffering

examples.x: When she heard about his loss, her heart went out to him, and she offered her support and a shoulder to lean on.
Seeing the elderly lady struggling to carry her groceries, my heart went out to her, and I offered to help carry them to her car.

senses.definition: to make someone feel strong emotions such as enthusiasm or sympathy

examples.x: The heartfelt speech by the cancer survivor struck a chord with everyone in the audience.
Her poignant poem about resilience and hope struck a chord with readers around the world.

senses.definition: a personality marked by emotional coldness and lack of sympathy

examples.x: Despite the heartbreaking situation, his heart of stone never allowed him to shed a tear.
She seemed to possess a heart of stone, showing no sympathy for the struggles of her coworkers.
Even in the face of the most touching stories, the manager maintained his reputation for having a heart of stone.
Her heart of stone was evident when she coldly rejected his heartfelt apology.

senses.definition: said to show sympathy for a person's difficult or unlucky situation
to make a person emotional, particularly by causing them to feel sympathy for someone

examples.x: The heartfelt letter from her old friend touched her heart and brought tears to her eyes.
The beautiful melody of the song always manages to touch my heart and make me feel nostalgic.
